pr.title: disassemble a bunch of data pointer tables
pr.author: RevoSucks

summary: PR 39 mass-disassembled pointer-table data in asm/data.s, replacing large .incbin spans with explicit .4byte entries, NULL placeholders, .global labels, and alignment directives. It also included macros.inc from data.s, added a NULL equate in include/macros.inc, and promoted several code addresses in asm/text_2.s to .global func_* labels so data tables could reference function boundaries. The work mainly improved visibility/searchability of data tables and function-pointer relationships rather than decompiling C code.

systems: fighter-related data tables inferred from FighterOnLoad_Peach and many 0x801x function pointers

what_changed: asm/data.s now includes macros.inc near the top so data declarations can use shared assembler constants such as NULL.

Large .incbin regions in asm/data.s were replaced with explicit pointer tables using .4byte entries, including tables at labels such as lbl_803C06E8, lbl_803C07AC, lbl_803C1154, lbl_803C11D8, lbl_803C125C, lbl_803C12E0, lbl_803C1364, and many subsequent lbl_803C**** symbols.

The existing lbl_803C1154 function-pointer table was retained/expanded in explicit form with entries such as func_800E0960, func_800E57AC, func_8010D9AC, FighterOnLoad_Peach, func_8011EF3C, and many other 0x801x fighter-function symbols.
Many table holes or zero entries were represented as .4byte NULL instead of raw zeroes after adding NULL to macros.inc.

asm/text_2.s gained .global func_* labels at previously unlabeled code starts, including func_8008A494, func_8008A644, func_8008A678, func_800BFE6C, func_800BFE70, func_800C95F4, func_800C9614, func_800C9768, func_800C9788, and multiple func_800D3xxx/func_800D5xxx labels.
include/macros.inc added a TODO comment near SDA constants and defined .set NULL, 0.
The PR was very large for its era: 3 changed files, 10525 added lines, 180 deleted lines, with most churn in asm/data.s.
smart_moves: Converted opaque baserom .incbin ranges into explicit .4byte pointer tables, making references grepable and enabling later symbol naming/decompilation work.
Used .global labels at each recovered data-table boundary to preserve addressability and make future table identification incremental rather than all-or-nothing.
Added missing text labels as global func_* symbols instead of leaving data entries as anonymous addresses where possible, improving cross-reference quality between data.s and text_2.s.
Introduced a shared NULL assembler constant and included macros.inc from data.s, reducing ambiguity for zero pointer slots in sparse tables.
Kept .balign 4 around table starts, preserving layout while replacing binary blobs with assembly declarations.
Mixed symbolic .4byte entries and raw absolute-address .4byte entries when symbol names were not yet available, allowing progress without blocking on full function identification.
decomp_lessons: For data-table recovery, exposing exact table contents in asm/data.s is valuable even before structs or semantic names are known; it turns opaque bytes into searchable pointer relationships.
When replacing .incbin with pointer data, preserve alignment and label boundaries first, then improve names later as functions and structs become known.
Sparse pointer tables should use a named zero/null constant where accepted by the assembler; this documents intent better than repeated 0x00000000 entries.
Adding function labels in text assembly may be necessary before data tables can be expressed symbolically; data work and text boundary recovery often unblock each other.
Large repetitive function-pointer tables can reveal subsystem organization and repeated per-character/per-state table shapes, even if the PR does not yet name the structure.
Avoid over-interpreting raw pointer tables: this PR provides many addresses and labels but little PR-body/review evidence about exact table semantics.
naming_conventions: Unknown data labels follow address-derived lbl_803C**** / lbl_803F**** style.
Unknown function labels follow address-derived func_800***** / func_801***** style.
Known functions already available remain semantic and case-sensitive, e.g. FighterOnLoad_Peach.
Null pointer slots are represented as NULL after defining .set NULL, 0.
Assembler labels are exported with .global before the label definition when they need cross-file references.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace exact .incbin spans with equivalent .4byte pointer lists while preserving byte size/order and .balign directives.
Use .include "macros.inc" in data assembly when shared constants are needed.
Represent function-pointer tables with .4byte func_* where a symbol exists; fall back to .4byte 0x800xxxxx or .4byte 0x803xxxxx when no symbol/name is available yet.
Add .global func_* labels at instruction addresses in asm/text_2.s to make recovered code-entry boundaries externally referencable from data tables.
Use .4byte NULL for zero pointer entries in sparse tables.
Do not require full C decompilation to improve matchability/searchability; data disassembly can be a standalone progress step.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the PR slice; no explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes are available.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename lbl_803C1154 or adjacent lbl_803C11D8/lbl_803C12E0 tables into typed fighter tables.
Search for later conversion of raw .4byte 0x800xxxxx entries from this PR into func_* symbols.
Trace uses of the added NULL macro in data.s and whether later cleanup removed the TODO in macros.inc.
Investigate whether the lbl_803C13E8 through lbl_803C1B20 table series corresponds to per-character action/state callbacks.
Check subsequent PRs touching asm/text_2.s around func_8008A494, func_800BFE6C, func_800C95F4, and func_800D3xxx labels for semantic names.
